result_summary: Kerr timelike geodesic validation successfully rebuilt (Contract 2). The previous null-geodesic and affine-parameter metrics are replaced with timelike orbits (H = -0.5) and integrated proper times. Sagnac-flat rotating frame (Langevin-Minkowski) with omega = a/r^3 acts as the strongest mundane comparator, perfectly matching Kerr's coordinate-time asymmetry. However, proper-time asymmetry curves remain clearly distinguishable. For spin parameters a/M in [0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9], the Kerr proper-time asymmetries are [0.009035, 0.027110, 0.045198, 0.063309, 0.081451] vs Sagnac asymmetries [0.006325, 0.018974, 0.031623, 0.044272, 0.056921]. This difference is 10^9 times larger than the numerical integration error bars (order of 10^-11), satisfying the failable distinction criterion.
strongest_mundane_comparator: Langevin-Minkowski rotating flat spacetime with frame rotation omega = a/r^3 matching Kerr coordinate-time asymmetry exactly.
independent_rederivation: Analytic Keplerian frequencies and proper-time dilation matched with high-precision 4th-order Runge-Kutta numerical integration of 4D timelike geodesics.
